arts.sum: Although the player has yet to be declared fully recovered this January 6 from the injury that he suffered on December 15 against Leganés, for which he was expected to be out for 3 to 4 weeks, he has nevertheless been included the list of players that are travelling to Saudi Arabia for the Spanish Super Cup. The team touched down in Jeddah, the country's second largest city, this Monday, ready to compete in the Spanish Super Cup, the four-team tournament that's happening from January 8 to 12.

arts.ttl: Nico Williams ‘unlikely’ to move in January amid Premier League interest
arts.sum: Nico Williams is reportedly not likely to make a blockbuster move to the Premier League in the January transfer window. The Athletic Club winger has attracted interest from some of Europe’s top clubs, including Barcelona, following his exploits for club and country last year. The 22-year-old helped the Bilbao outfit claim their first Copa del […] The post Nico Williams ‘unlikely’ to move in January amid Premier League interest first appeared on The Football Faithful.

arts.ttl: Dani Olmo affected by Barcelona registration disaster but continues to reject interest
arts.sum: Barcelona did not do much business in the summer, but having brought in the player with the most goal contributions at Euro 2024, there was a feeling that signing Dani Olmo could help them to make the jump to the next level. However they have not made life easy for him. arts.ttl: When is the Copa del Rey last 16 draw and who could Barca face?
arts.sum: Hansi Flick’s side will now find out their next opponents on Wednesday, January 8 when the draw for the next round is made at 12pm CET. There were plenty of shocks again in the third round with Las Palmas, Real Valladolid and Sevilla all knocked out of the tournament.
